summary:
## Metadata
- File path: package.json
- File name: package.json
- Extension: .json
- Inferred language/type: JSON package manifest for a Node.js/CommonJS project
- Content hash: 50212192abc450e8215513f06ceeb792
- Line count: 53
- Byte size: 1361
- Summary format: structured-functional-v1
- Notable symbols/classes/functions: name, version, description, license, author, homepage, bugs, repository, type, main, bin, files, engines, scripts, dependencies, devDependencies
- External dependencies: @modelcontextprotocol/sdk, commander, ignore, openai, simple-git, vectra, zod, @types/node, typescript, zod-to-json-schema
- Internal dependencies: dist/index.js, dist/mcp.js, dist/scripts/generate-schemas.js, dist, schemas, README.md, LICENSE, .diffdocrc.example
- Public API/exports: CLI executables `diffdoc` and `diffdoc-mcp`; package main entry `dist/index.js`

## Purpose
Defines the Node.js package metadata, runtime requirements, published files, scripts, and dependency set for the `diffdoc` project. It configures the package as a CommonJS application with CLI entry points and build/prepare workflows.

## User-Visible Behavior
Users installing the package get two command-line executables: `diffdoc` and `diffdoc-mcp`.
Running package scripts can:
- build the project and generate schemas
- regenerate schemas only
- clean the `dist` directory
- start the main application after compiling
- run the build automatically during package preparation/publish lifecycle

Consumers of the package will resolve the main module from `dist/index.js`.

## Business Rules
- The package name is `diffdoc` and the version is `0.6.0`.
- The project is licensed under MIT.
- The package is intended to run on Node.js `>=22`.
- The package is treated as CommonJS via `"type": "commonjs"`.
- Only the listed files are intended for package publication.
- `prepare` must run `npm run build`.
- `build` must compile TypeScript before generating schemas.
- `start` must compile TypeScript before launching `dist/index.js`.
- CLI command names are fixed to `diffdoc` and `diffdoc-mcp`.

## Data Inputs And Outputs
Inputs:
- Package metadata fields in this JSON file
- Build/runtime scripts referenced in `scripts`
- Dependency declarations for runtime and development tooling

Outputs:
- npm package metadata used for installation and publication
- CLI binaries mapped to built JavaScript files
- Main module resolution to `dist/index.js`
- Build artifacts expected under `dist`
- Generated schema artifacts expected under `schemas`

## Side Effects
- `build` compiles TypeScript and runs schema generation.
- `generate:schemas` runs the schema generation script.
- `clean` deletes the `dist` directory recursively and forcefully.
- `start` compiles TypeScript and then executes the built app.
- `prepare` triggers the build during npm lifecycle events.

## Error And Edge Cases
- If Node.js is below version 22, the package declares itself unsupported.
- If `dist/index.js` or `dist/mcp.js` is missing, the CLI binaries will not resolve correctly.
- If TypeScript compilation fails, `build`, `start`, and `prepare` fail.
- If schema generation fails, `build` and `generate:schemas` fail.
- `clean` is forceful and may remove `dist` even if it contains stale or partially built artifacts.
- Package consumers relying on files outside the `files` allowlist may not receive them in published installs.

## Dependencies
External:
- `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` for MCP-related functionality
- `commander` for CLI parsing
- `ignore` for ignore-pattern handling
- `openai` for model/API integration
- `simple-git` for Git repository interaction
- `vectra` for vector storage/indexing
- `zod` for schema validation
- `typescript` and `@types/node` for development/build tooling
- `zod-to-json-schema` for schema conversion

Internal:
- Built outputs in `dist/index.js`, `dist/mcp.js`, and `dist/scripts/generate-schemas.js`
- Published schema assets under `schemas`
- Repository documentation and example config files listed in `files`

## Operational Notes
- The package is configured for a compiled distribution model rather than direct source execution.
- `prepare` ensures builds happen automatically in install/publish workflows, which can increase install time.
- The `files` allowlist suggests the published artifact set is intentionally limited to build outputs and supporting docs/config examples.
- Schema generation is part of the normal build path, so schema drift should be addressed by rerunning the build.
- The CLI entry points depend on successful TypeScript compilation into `dist`.
